message:
feat(tailer): global streaming health surface and chaos module visibility

tailer.rs:
- Add global StreamingHealth snapshot surface via OnceLock<StdRwLock>
  (same pattern as runtime lock/memory telemetry). StreamingBridge now
  auto-publishes a health snapshot after each process_event() and
  record_fallback() call.
- StreamingHealth::update_global() / get_global(): write/read the
  latest streaming health snapshot from any thread. Enables the IPC
  status endpoint and diagnostic surfaces to report streaming capture
  health without holding a reference to the bridge.
- StreamingHealth: add serde Serialize/Deserialize derives for JSON
  status payloads.
- TailerMode: add serde Serialize/Deserialize derives with
  rename_all="snake_case" for consistent JSON representation.
- snapshot_health(): new private method on StreamingBridge that
  constructs a StreamingHealth from current bridge state.

chaos.rs:
- Remove #[cfg(test)] gate from chaos module so it can be used in
  integration tests and benchmarks, not just unit tests. The module
  provides fault injection scenarios that are valuable for resilience
  testing outside the crate's own test harness.
- Fix wildcard import in scenarios submodule: replace `use super::*`
  with explicit `use super::{ChaosAssertion, ChaosScenario, FaultMode,
  FaultPoint}` to satisfy clippy::wildcard_imports.

lib.rs:
- Remove #[cfg(test)] attribute from `pub mod chaos` to match the
  chaos.rs visibility change above.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
